evidence: ssl.create_default_context() followed by check_hostname=False and verify_mode=ssl.CERT_NONE at lines 379-380 (repeats at 943-944, 1152-1153, 1210-1211); gated by validate_local_host() at line 329 which requires a private/loopback/link-local address per is_local_address() at line 308
description: TLS certificate verification is disabled by default for the printer's MQTT/FTPS sockets, mitigated by a private-host-only guard for LAN-only Bambu firmware self-signed certs
suggested_fix: Document the CERT_NONE + validate_local_host() pairing in a code comment at each call site, or move to certificate-fingerprint pinning (TOFU) via the existing serial-fetch path

evidence: Line 83: curl --header "Authorization: Bearer $VERCEL_TOKEN" sent to https://api.vercel.com/v9/projects; VERCEL_TOKEN is read from environment at line 68
description: Vercel deploy token is transmitted via Authorization header to Vercel's own API — correct, expected CI/CD usage
fp_reason: Token is sent only to the API it authenticates against (Vercel's own endpoint), over HTTPS, in a workflow that requires the token to already be present in the environment; this is the intended deployment flow, not exfiltration
rule_gap: The MEDIUM 'environment variable access + network calls' heuristic can't distinguish a token being sent to its own issuing service from exfiltration to an unrelated endpoint; a rule refinement could allowlist same-vendor API-domain matches (e.g. token name prefix matches API host)

evidence: ezdxf, playwright, networkx, lxml, pytest, trimesh listed with zero version constraint across requirements-dev.txt and 15 sibling requirements.txt files
description: Third-party Python dependencies across all requirements.txt files in the repo have no version constraint
suggested_fix: Add minimum version bounds (e.g. ezdxf>=1.3) to guard against breaking upstream releases
